page: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is a memory-intensive virtual machine instance belonging to the Famsv7 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The configuration features 2 physical cores and 2 dedicated vCPUs, with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 32.0 GB per core. The instance has no local storage, relying instead on external premium storage options, and does not include any GPU hardware.

Benchmark data highlights strong single-core performance alongside moderate multi-core capabilities. In stress-ng single-core CPU testing, the instance achieves a top-tier rating in the top 10%, which is mirrored by top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, multi-threaded performance is more limited due to the 2-core configuration; multi-core stress-ng CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall into the average range, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is rated as weak. Memory bandwidth benchmarks for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks consistently register in the average tier.

The high memory density relative to the low core count presents a specific resource tradeoff. While database operations, Redis SET operations, and small-model LLM inference perform in the average tier, web serving throughput is weak, and medium-model LLM prompt processing falls into the poor category. This profile makes the instance suitable for memory-heavy, single-threaded or low-concurrency workloads, such as small-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and lightweight data processing tasks that require substantial RAM but do not scale across many CPU cores.
description: The Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the Famsv7 family, configured with 2 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. It features a high memory allocation of 64.0 GB (32.0 GB per core), with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-threaded capabilities and web serving throughput are weaker due to the dual-core limit. Memory bandwidth, database operations, and Redis SET performance remain in the average tier. This qualitative resource profile offers high memory density relative to compute power, making it suitable for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads such as caching layers, small in-memory databases, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is a memory-intensive VM featuring 2 dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F8-2ams_v7 is an x86_64 VM with 2 dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC vCPUs and 64 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive dual-core virtual machine featuring high-frequency dedicated processors and substantial memory capacity for low-concurrency, RAM-heavy workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a dedicated 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 2 physical cores.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 32.0 GB per core ratio.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression benchmark performance.
No local storage or GPU hardware included.
Suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency workloads like caching and small databases.
